data.title: AI TRENDS | Meta CEO Says AI Agent Development Has Not Accelerated as Expected
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META.O) CEO said that over the past four months, the development of AI agents has not “accelerated as we expected.”

According to Jin10, the CEO made the remarks while describing the pace of progress in AI agent development.

data.title: Meta Shares Fall 4.89% In Short-Term Trading
data.description: Meta Platforms (META.O) fell slightly in short-term trading, last down 4.89%. According to Jin10, the stock’s latest decline was 4.89%.

data.title: Securitize tokenizes $295 million of its own stock on Solana and Avalanche amid NYSE debut
data.description: Securitize tokenized $295 million of its own stock on Solana and Avalanche as it debuted on the New York Stock Exchange, creating what it called the largest issuer-sponsored tokenized stock at launch. The move is aimed at differentiating Securitize from rival third-party tokenized stock issuers, according to CoinDesk.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister: Regional Peace Requires Comprehensive, Inclusive Approach Free of External Interference
data.description: Iran’s foreign minister said peace in the region can be maintained only if it is comprehensive, inclusive, and free from external interference. According to Jin10, the minister described these conditions as necessary for sustaining regional peace.

data.title: AI | Rep. Jason Smith Says US Must Lead in AI, Not China
data.description:
Missouri Representative Jason Smith said the US needs to ensure it remains the leader in artificial intelligence rather than China, according to Bloomberg.

Smith, a Republican, made the comments in an interview on Bloomberg Television’s Balance of Power, where he also discussed the one-year anniversary of the One Big Beautiful Bill and recent news involving OpenAI.

Asked about the USMCA, Smith said the Trump administration is seeking a better trading relationship through its decision not to renew its trade pact with Canada and Mexico.

data.title: Sen. Warren Pushes to Bar U.S. President Donald Trump and Family From Crypto Profits in Senate Bill
data.description:
Senator Elizabeth Warren is seeking to add a provision to pending Senate cryptocurrency legislation that would ban U.S. President Donald Trump, his family, and senior officials from profiting from crypto.

According to NS3.AI, Trump’s financial disclosure reported that his crypto ventures generated more than $1.4 billion in his first year back in office.

A floor vote on the Senate market-structure bill has not been scheduled.

data.title: Bond Market’s Fed Hike Outlook Shifts After Jobs Data, Oil Prices
data.description:
US government bonds ended the holiday-shortened week with lower short-term yields after June employment data challenged expectations for Federal Reserve rate hikes this year, according to Bloomberg.

The shift in rate expectations came as investors also weighed moves in oil prices.

data.title: Cloudflare Flags Solana-Based Prediction Market World for Suspected Phishing
data.description:
Cloudflare flagged the Solana-based prediction market World for suspected phishing after World Network accused the site of brand impersonation.

According to NS3.AI, World launched yesterday, and its landing page now shows a warning indicating the site may be a potential phishing risk.

The report said Tools for Humanity asked Cloudflare to take the site down, citing an apparent email that World shared.

data.title: Erik Voorhees Defends Venice’s $65 Million Series A Token Deal After VVV Pricing Criticism
data.description:
Erik Voorhees defended the terms of Venice’s $65 million Series A token deal after critics argued the structure underpriced the VVV token.

According to NS3.AI, Voorhees said investors could ultimately pay up to $131 million for 6.5 million locked VVV tokens if they choose to exercise an attached option.

The financing structure includes 1.5 million VVV tokens that vest, along with an option to purchase an additional 5 million VVV tokens. Voorhees said all tokens in the deal are locked for four years.

data.title: Tokenized Stock Collateral Exploit at Edel Inflates wGOOGLx Value 78-Fold, Firm Says Depositors Won’t Lose Funds
data.description:
Edel disclosed a $403,000 exploit involving tokenized stock collateral and said no depositor would bear losses.

According to NS3.AI, the attacker manipulated the wGOOGLx-to-GOOGLx exchange rate, inflating the collateral value of wGOOGLx to roughly 78 times its correct level.

Edel said security firms produced different loss estimates because they used different measurement approaches, including bad debt, gross loss, or attacker profit.

data.title: Irish Authorities Recover 500 BTC Linked to Convicted Drug Trafficker Clifton Collins
data.description:
Irish authorities recovered 500 BTC worth nearly $31 million from cryptocurrency holdings linked to convicted drug trafficker Clifton Collins.

According to NS3.AI, Ireland’s Criminal Assets Bureau said the latest seizure brings the total recovery in the case to 1,500 BTC worth over $92 million.

Separately, Arkham Intelligence data indicates wallets tagged “Clifton Collins” still hold roughly 4,500 BTC.

data.title: ENS DAO Ends Public Goods Working Group After Four and a Half Years of Grants
data.description:
ENS DAO has sunset its Public Goods Working Group, ending a grants program that ran for four and a half years.

According to NS3.AI, Simona Pop said the group’s final term committed $450,000 in USDC and 72.5 ETH across Builder Grants, Strategic Grants, and advocacy work.

No new funding round has been announced to replace the grants pipeline.

data.title: WORLD CUP | Tuchel says Declan Rice has no injury ahead of England vs Mexico last-16
data.description: England midfielder Declan Rice has no injury and is expected to be fit for Sunday’s World Cup last-16 match against Mexico at the Azteca, coach Thomas Tuchel said. Rice went off late in England’s 2-1 win over the Democratic Republic of Congo after managing nerve pain in his back during the tournament, and Tuchel said the issue was pain rather than an injury, according to Yahoo Sports.

data.title: Refiner Profit Margins Surge as Hormuz Shipping Snarls Linger
data.description:
US crude refiners are posting some of their best profit margins in years, signaling that supply-chain disruptions tied to the Strait of Hormuz are still affecting global markets, according to Bloomberg.

The gains come even as energy shipments through the strait have started to pick up, suggesting lingering logistical kinks continue to support refining profitability.

data.title: Securitize Begins NYSE Trading Under SECZ and Lists Common Stock Onchain
data.description:
Securitize began trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker SECZ on Thursday and placed its newly listed common stock onchain the same day. According to NS3.AI, eligible U.S. investors can access tokenized SECZ through Securitize’s regulated platform after completing onboarding, KYC, AML, and jurisdiction checks.

The SECZ listing followed a merger with Cantor Equity Partners, which brought roughly $400 million in proceeds. Securitize said tokenized SECZ will launch on Avalanche and Solana.

data.title: Polymarket Bettor With $570,000 Weekly Profit Holds $360,000 Position on Portugal vs. Croatia Goals
data.description:
A Polymarket participant who made $570,000 in profit over the past week holds a $360,000 position predicting more than 2.5 total goals in the “World Cup Round of 16: Portugal vs. Croatia” market.

According to Odaily, monitoring by a PPP prediction-market tool identified the address associated with the position as 0x10a6fadcbacd66330862206f6199b197e3ad4d8b.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Carney to Keep North BC Tanker Ban as Alberta to Unveil Pipeline Proposal
data.description:
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said he will keep the ban on oil tankers off northern British Columbia as Alberta prepares to unveil a pipeline proposal, according to Bloomberg.

Carney also announced billions of dollars in federal funding for energy, mining and transportation projects in British Columbia.

data.title: Saylor Pits Strategy Against Mag 7 as MSTR Options Open Interest Hits 71.9% of Market Cap
data.description:
Michael Saylor compared Strategy (MSTR) with the Magnificent 7 on July 2, calling it Wall Street’s “MoST inteResting” stock as shares rebound from last week’s lows. According to BeInCrypto, a chart Saylor shared shows MSTR options open interest at 71.9% of market cap, versus Tesla (TSLA) at 15.8% and Meta (META) at 10.8%.

Strategy’s latest filing shows 847,363 Bitcoin (BTC) bought for $64.1 billion at an average $75,646 per coin; with BTC near $61,760, the stake is about $54 billion. MSTR rose 12.5% Monday after a capital management overhaul, then fell 6.2% Tuesday to $86.93 as TD Cowen cut its target to $260 from $400; Citi kept Buy but cut its target to $136. Rosen Law Firm opened a securities probe into Strategy.

data.title: Germany Should Pause Coal Plant Closures, Grid Operator Amprion Says
data.description: Germany should halt further closures of coal-fired power plants for now to safeguard electricity supplies as renewable generation expands, according to Bloomberg, citing grid operator Amprion GmbH.

data.title: Iranian President Says Supporting Armed Forces Is His Duty, Iranian Media Reports
data.description:
Iranian media outlet Fars News reported that Iran’s president said supporting the armed forces was his duty and that he would support them with all his strength.

According to Jin10, the report attributed the remarks to Iran’s president.
